CARDAMOM CHAI TEA

There's nothing quite like the warming embrace of a proper chai on a cold winter's day.
This spiced tea, inspired by the tea stalls of India and perfected over many afternoons
spent in comfortable kitchens, brings together the warmth of spices with the comfort of
milk. The cardamom pods are the star here—bruised just slightly to release their oils,
they infuse the entire pot with an intoxicating aroma. My grandmother used to make this
every Sunday morning, and I remember the kitchen filling with this spicy, sweet steam
before we'd even sit down to breakfast. It's a recipe meant for sharing, for slowing
down, and for enjoying life's simple pleasures.

Ingredients:
4 cups water
4 green cardamom pods, slightly crushed
4 black peppercorns
2 cinnamon sticks, about 3 inches each
4 whole cloves
1 inch piece fresh ginger, sliced
4 black tea bags or 4 teaspoons loose black tea
2 tablespoons honey or sugar
3/4 cup whole milk
Pinch of sea salt

Method:
Bring water to a boil in a pot. Add cardamom pods, peppercorns, cinnamon,
cloves, and ginger. Simmer for 5 minutes to infuse the spices.

Add black tea and simmer for another 3-4 minutes. Don't over-steep or the tea
will become bitter. Stir in honey and salt to taste.

Warm the milk separately and gently pour into each cup along with the spiced tea.
Strain the spices through a fine mesh if desired, though some prefer them left in.

Serves 4. Best enjoyed hot with biscuits or pastries.
